judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both reports hit all four expectations with grounded detail; Report 2 is marginally more explicit on the reproducibility check (regenerate then re-run twice) while Report 1 is equally thorough overall.

report_1.evidence: `_live_call()` makes a real network request via `urllib.request.urlopen` to `https://api.scorelab-models.example.com/v1/score` ... make real **billable** API calls ... That is precisely why they are gated behind `-m "not live"`. If you ever want to check the live model, run `pytest -m live` out-of-band with the key set

report_2.evidence: each one POSTs to a real remote endpoint ... every run makes real, billable API calls — a surprise-cost risk ... The deselection is the intended design (an opt-in paid suite), not a skip to be "fixed." Keep the default run at `-m "not live"`; run the live suite only deliberately

report_1.evidence: **The diff contains an out-of-scope change.** ... Blindly regenerating silently bakes this in and hides it. Decide intent first ... re-run the default suite and confirm the golden diff shows **only** the intended field changes.

report_2.evidence: Before regenerating I read the diff and confirm every changed golden hunk traces to my intended change — and it does not ... Only once every remaining golden delta is explained by an intended change do I regenerate

report_1.evidence: Do not quote `docs/testing.md`; both figures there are stale/wrong versus the repo, so measure them

report_2.evidence: Not by quoting `docs/testing.md`. That doc is a stale snapshot and both figures are wrong when I re-measure them myself on this checkout

report_1.evidence: **The regen script is non-deterministic.** `scripts/update_golden.py` iterates a **set** (`CATEGORIES`) and writes `json.dumps(out, indent=2)` **without `sort_keys`**, so the JSON key order depends on the process hash seed ... after fixing the script to iterate `ITEMS`/add `sort_keys=True`. Then re-run the default suite and confirm the golden diff shows **only** the intended field changes.

report_2.evidence: regen, then re-run twice in assert mode to confirm determinism ... it iterates the `set` `CATEGORIES` ... I confirmed the iteration order changes with `PYTHONHASHSEED`, so consecutive regens can emit JSON keys in different orders → noisy, non-reproducible diffs
